name: Leptos
description: Fine-grained signals, full-stack with server functions and islands, one of the most active Rust web frameworks

description.value: Full-stack, fine-grained reactive Rust web framework with signals, server functions, and islands, inspired by SolidJS
description.comment: GitHub description: "Build fast web applications with Rust."

license.comment: crates.io version metadata reports license MIT (not dual-licensed).

documentation.comment: Official Leptos book; API docs also at docs.rs/leptos.

latest-stable-version.comment: Latest stable per crates.io as of 2026-07-19; a 0.9.0-beta prerelease was published 2026-07-18.

latest-release.comment: Date of the 0.8.20 stable release. Most recent crates.io publish overall is 0.9.0-beta on 2026-07-18 (prerelease).

maintenance-status.comment: Frequent releases (multiple in 2026) and commits pushed within the last day as of 2026-07-19.

maturity.comment: Still pre-1.0 (0.8.x stable line, 0.9 in beta); API stabilizing and production-usable but not yet 1.0.

corporate-foundation-backing.comment: Open-source community project (leptos-rs org) funded largely via GitHub Sponsors; no single corporate owner.

reactivity-model.comment: Fine-grained reactivity via signals (SolidJS-inspired); a signal change updates the exact affected node without re-running components.

view-authoring.comment: Primary authoring is the JSX-like view! macro; a builder API also exists (counter_without_macros example).

fine-grained-updates.comment: Signal changes target exact DOM nodes; no full-subtree re-render.

uses-virtual-dom.comment: No virtual DOM; fine-grained reactive updates instead of diffing.

async-await-in-components.comment: Async data via Resource + <Suspense>; server functions and resources are awaitable.

global-state-solution.comment: provide_context/use_context plus shared signals/stores for app-wide state.

compiles-to.comment: Compiles to WebAssembly via wasm-bindgen for the client.

primary-build-tool.comment: cargo-leptos is the official full-stack build tool; Trunk is recommended for pure client-side (CSR) apps.

official-cli.comment: cargo-leptos is a first-party CLI for scaffolding, building, and serving full-stack apps.

dev-server-hmr.comment: cargo-leptos watch provides live reload; experimental subsecond hot-patching (subsecond_hot_patch example) plus view/HTML hot reload.

minimal-wasm-bundle-size.comment: No independently reproduced hello-world .wasm gzipped measurement under a consistent profile; omitted per RESEARCH.md rather than citing unmethodical numbers.

js-interop.comment: Standard wasm-bindgen stack (web-sys/js-sys) for browser and JS interop.

js-interop-ergonomics.comment: Works through the standard wasm-bindgen/web-sys stack with the usual boilerplate; no bespoke ergonomic layer.

no-std-minimal-runtime.comment: Requires std; not a no_std framework.

client-side-rendering-spa.comment: Pure CSR mode supported (e.g. counter, todo_app_sqlite_csr, tailwind_csr examples), built with Trunk.

static-site-generation.comment: Static rendering supported (static_routing example; ssr_modes include static routes).

hydration.comment: Full hydration plus out-of-order/in-order streaming (progressive) and an islands mode (book Section 18; islands and hackernews_islands_axum examples).

server-functions.comment: Isomorphic #[server] functions callable from client code with wiring generated (book Section 15.1).

streaming-ssr.comment: HTTP streaming of HTML with out-of-order or in-order streaming of Suspense components.

backend-integration.comment: First-party leptos_axum and leptos_actix integrations; Axum and Actix starter templates.

isomorphic-routing.comment: leptos_router runs identically on server and client.

official-router.comment: leptos_router is maintained in-tree by the core team.

nested-routes.comment: Book Section 9 covers nested routing with <Outlet/>.

typed-routes.comment: Routes are path-based; the path! macro helps but routes are not enum/derive-based compile-checked in the sense of an invalid route being a type error.

route-guards.comment: No dedicated navigation-guard hook; access control is done via redirects/<Redirect> inside route views.

lazy-code-split-routes.comment: Lazy/code-split route loading demonstrated in the lazy_routes example (wasm code splitting).

styling-approach.comment: Plain CSS via <Stylesheet>; Tailwind first-class (tailwind_csr, tailwind_axum, tailwind_actix examples). Scoped CSS available via community crates (e.g. stylance), not built-in.

scoped-styles.comment: No built-in component-scoped styling; achievable via third-party crates like stylance.

component-library-ecosystem.comment: A few community UI kits (e.g. Thaw UI, Leptonic) plus leptos-use utilities; not as broad as mature JS ecosystems.

forms-inputs.comment: Controlled inputs, bind: two-way binding, and <ActionForm>/<Form> helpers (book Section 16.1; action-form example).

svg-canvas-support.comment: SVG elements authored directly in the view! macro; canvas via web-sys interop.

error-boundaries.comment: <ErrorBoundary> for fallback UI (book Section 3.8; error_boundary example).

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive official book (CSR through deployment), API docs on docs.rs, and 40+ examples; occasionally trails the newest release.

api-ergonomics.comment: Idiomatic signals-based API with the view! macro; some learning curve around reactive ownership, but low boilerplate overall.

compile-times.comment: Macro-heavy full-stack builds are non-trivial but comparable to peer Rust web frameworks for a small app.

examples-count.comment: 42 example projects in the official repo examples/ directory as of 2026-07-19.

interactive-playground.comment: No official online REPL/playground; the repo examples/ gallery serves that role.

browser-devtools.comment: No dedicated first-party component/signal inspector beyond generic browser devtools.

testing-support.comment: wasm-bindgen-test for unit tests; end-to-end examples use Playwright (e.g. counter, todo examples ship e2e/ dirs).

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads of the leptos crate as of 2026-07-19.

contributors.comment: Approximate; GitHub contributors API pagination reports ~381 (including anonymous) as of 2026-07-19.

community-size.comment: Active Discord community and 380+ contributors; one of the most active Rust web frameworks.

ecosystem-crates.comment: Routers in-tree plus community crates (leptos-use, Thaw UI, Leptonic, leptos-query); expanding but still maturing.

notable-production-users.comment: No widely-documented named production users verified from primary sources; omitted rather than citing marketing/demos.

meta-framework-full-stack-kit.value: Built-in full-stack mode via cargo-leptos (leptos_axum / leptos_actix)
meta-framework-full-stack-kit.comment: Full-stack is first-party rather than a separate meta-framework; cargo-leptos orchestrates SSR + server functions.
